component.name: Speed of Sound
component.summary: Voice typing for the Linux desktop

description.p: Speed of Sound is a voice-typing app for the Linux desktop. Press a key, speak your text, and it types itself into any focused application.

By default, transcription runs on-device using Whisper, so no audio ever leaves your machine. The app comes with a built-in multilingual Whisper Tiny model and works out of the box. Additional models can be downloaded from within the app to improve accuracy.

ul.li: Offline, private transcription with the bundled multilingual Whisper Tiny model
Types directly into any app via XDG Desktop Portals (supports X11 and Wayland)
Optional text polishing with LLMs, supports self-hosted services like Ollama, vLLM, and llama.cpp
Optional cloud providers supported for resource-constrained hardware environments

description.p: Added Gemini 3.1 Pro and Gemini Flash Lite as available cloud text models, replacing Gemini 3 Pro. Also improved LLM text polishing quality with few-shot prompt examples for better handling of commonly spoken language patterns. Fixed icon visibility for model removal on light mode.

New Import/Export page in Preferences lets you back up your full configuration (API keys, LLM providers, vocabulary, and language settings) to a JSON file and restore it on any machine. Also improved the LLM text polishing prompt with better handling of capitalization, punctuation, and spoken abbreviations.

Fixed two crashes: one on startup caused by a threading issue in the main view, and one during shutdown in the audio pipeline. When a previously configured ASR model is no longer available, the app now automatically falls back to the default Whisper model instead of crashing. Built-in models no longer show a delete button in the Voice Models preferences page.
Lowered the minimum required JDK from 25 to 22, making the app easier to run on more systems without needing a bleeding-edge Java installation.

Fixed a crash on fresh installations where no ASR model had been configured yet. The app now falls back to the default Whisper model automatically on the first run. Also expanded the FAQ with coverage of privacy, microphone selection, model sizes, and Wayland/X11 compatibility.

First release of Speed of Sound. Captures microphone audio, transcribes it on-device using Whisper via Sherpa ONNX, and types the result into any focused application using XDG Desktop Portal keyboard simulation. Optional LLM text polishing is supported via Anthropic, Google, OpenAI, and compatible self-hosted endpoints such as Ollama, vLLM, and llama.cpp.
